topic: A college student discovers their roommate has been reading their journal
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence in the room was suddenly thick, suffocating. I was halfway through sketching a particularly chaotic cityscape, the charcoal smudging my fingertips, when I noticed it. The open page of my journal, the one I’d meticulously hidden under my pillow, was sitting on my desk, face down, but the spine was slightly bent. My stomach clenched, a tight, cold knot forming beneath my ribs. I slowly turned it over. The handwriting—my handwriting—was there, but interspersed between my own frantic scribbles were neat, precise annotations, observations about my anxieties, things I hadn't even written yet. When Liam walked in, whistling tunelessly, I didn't look up. I just tightened my grip on my pencil until my knuckles were white, the pressure a physical manifestation of the simmering resentment bubbling up inside me. "Everything alright?" he asked, his tone too light, too casual. I kept my eyes fixed on the paper, refusing to acknowledge the shift in the atmosphere, letting the heavy silence stretch until he finally backed out, leaving me alone with the undeniable evidence of his trespass.

[story 2]

It happened with the insidious quiet of a slow leak. I found the lock on my desk drawer ajar this morning. My breath hitched. Inside, nestled amongst my pens and dried-out markers, was my diary. Not just open, but actively being read. A section detailing my frustration with Professor Albright’s grading was underlined with a faint, almost invisible pencil mark. My jaw locked so hard I could feel the strain in my molars. I stood up abruptly, the chair scraping loudly against the floor, a deliberate, jarring sound meant to shatter the fragile peace. "What are you doing?" I managed, my voice sharper than intended, laced with a brittle edge. My gaze was locked onto Liam, who was lounging on his bed, scrolling through his phone with an infuriating air of nonchalance. I didn't wait for an answer. I snatched the journal, my fingers brushing against the page, and slammed the drawer shut with enough force to make the cheap wood shudder. I walked over to my side of the room, planting my hands on my hips, letting the stillness of my posture speak volumes about the deep, simmering annoyance that was boiling just beneath the surface of my skin.

[story 3]

The realization hit me like a physical blow when I found the corner of my notebook exposed. It wasn't just open; it was actively being scrutinized. Liam was sitting on the edge of my bed, ostensibly reading a textbook, but his eyes kept flicking down, lingering on the ink of my private thoughts. A heat rushed up my neck, making my skin prickle with anger. I felt a sudden, intense need to push him away, to create distance, to make him understand the sheer audacity of his intrusion. I didn't speak immediately. Instead, I picked up a heavy textbook from my shelf and deliberately placed it on top of my desk, a clear, solid barrier. My breathing became shallow, controlled, every muscle in my body coiled tight. When he finally looked up, sensing the sudden tension, I met his gaze squarely. My expression was carefully blank, a mask of cool indifference, but the slight tremor in my hand as I adjusted the book was enough to broadcast the sheer refusal in my posture. "Mind your business," I stated, the words coming out low and steady, each syllable weighted with a stubborn refusal to yield ground.
